entities.name: Careerspan

entities.name: Emory

entities.name: Quinicia
entities.context: Low-cost AI tool for resumes and career readiness that universities are tempted by

entities.name: Narrow Referral Graph
entities.context: Over-reliance on a limited referral network that slows hiring and hides strong candidates

entities.name: High-Signal Inbound
entities.context: Careerspan's value proposition as a second engine of qualified candidate flow beyond referrals

entities.name: University Pilot Model
entities.context: Strategy to offer Careerspan free during a pilot in exchange for distribution, endorsement, and alumni access

entities.name: Internal Recruiters
entities.context: Default hiring mechanism companies fall back to after concluding external tools don't work

entities.name: Referrals
entities.context: Primary hiring channel that employers default to, forming a narrow graph

relationships.context: Careerspan is positioned as the vehicle to unlock high-signal inbound alongside existing referral channels
Careerspan addresses the limitation of narrow referral graphs by providing a complementary candidate engine
Emory is a target university partner that conceptually likes Careerspan but faces budget and process constraints
Emory and similar universities are tempted by Quinicia as a low-cost AI alternative for career readiness
